content.full_text:
'90-08-15 15:01 DAN.WONGEM.D&213-563-5704

[CITY LOGO]

CITY
CIVIC CENTER -
CERRIOS, CALIF

Post-it* brand fax transmittal memo 787

# of pages: 2
From: [illegible] (Gallucci)
To: [illegible]
cc: Cerritos
Phone: 860-0311
Dept:
Fax #:

August 15, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
Ms. Peggy Bernardi, Esq.
Legal Division
428 "J" Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Dear Ms. Bernardi:

I am an elected official in the City of Cerritos and have a specific question as it relates to Proposition 73. Our City's newsletter, which is printed at public expense, carried an article (see attached) that listed a Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) class in which I volunteered as an instructor. This class has been operating for twelve years and during that time we have instructed in excess of 3,000 individuals in the technique of CPR. The twelfth anniversary of this class is upon us, and I would like to do an article in the City newsletter advertising for individuals that have taken the class and might have saved a life to come to an anniversary party where they may be recognized for this humanitarian effort.

As the City newsletter is printed at public expense, I would like to know if this twelfth anniversary article would be permissible under the provision of Proposition 73. Given that I volunteered and received no remuneration for this program, it would appear that restricting the advertising of this activity with my name in said advertising is not what the legislature intended.

For your information, our City Charter has a two-term limit, and as I am at the end of my term and cannot run for City Council again without allowing two years to pass, it would be my opinion that prohibiting the use of my name in the article would not be in the spirit of the law.


ANN JO'INT                PAUL J. DEWLEN          JOHN J. [illegible]          SHERMAN D. KAPPE          DANIEL K. WONG M.D.
MAYOR                    VICE MAYOR          COUNCILMEMBER          COUNCILMEMBER          COUNCILMEMBER

I would appreciate your interpreting the law as it relates to
my request and advising me of your opinion. Should you have
any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me. I look
forward to hearing from you soon.

Sincerely,

Daniel K. Wong, M.D.
Councilmember

P.S. "Further information":
1) I personally have zero direct involvement
with the World Newsletter. Send to step or writing.
2) No photo, No headline is main feature.
3) This is a program I initiated, target
for 10 years and has been on-going program
listed in the Newsletter in the past.
4) This CPR promotion benefit the college
to learn to save life and will not
benefit my elected position as stated
above. Please reply by Fax, my Fax # 868-[?]04
Area code 213 our Newsletter article
deadline for September is 8/18/90.
And our CPR Anniversary date is
Sept 18th. But we need your interpretation
of just this article to promote CPR
this week to make it to the print
Shop. Fax your reply please.
Thank you.
Jean Uppum

(My phone # is
213 868-6888)

PAGE.001

AUG 15 1990 15:06

DATE:UM RECEIVED 08 AUG 06-21-200N
S0-4

Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Ms. Bernardi: I am an elected official in the City of Cerritos and have a specific question as it relates to Proposition 73. Our City's newsletter, which is printed at public expense, carried articles (see attached) that listed a Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) class in which I volunteered as an instructor. This class has been operating for twelve years and during that time we have instructed in excess of 3,000 individuals in the technique of CPR. The twelfth anniversary of this class is upon us, and I would like to do an article in the City newsletter advertising for individuals that have taken the class and might have saved a life to come to an anniversary party where they may be recognized for this humanitarian effort. As the City newsletter is printed at public expense, I would like to know if this twelfth anniversary article would be permissible under the provision of Proposition 73. Given that I volunteered and received no remuneration for this program, it would appear that restricting the advertising of this activity with my name in said advertising is not what the legislature intended. For your information, our City Charter has a two-term limit, and as I am at the end of my term and cannot run for City Council again without allowing two years to pass, it would be my opinion that prohibiting the use of my name in the article would not be in the spirit of the law. I would appreciate your interpreting the law as it relates to my request and advising me of your opinion. Should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Sincerely, Daniel K. Wong, M.D. Councilmember P.S. "Further information: 1). I personally have no direct involvement with the Monthly Newsletter such as editing or writing. 2). No photo, no headline as main feature. 3). This is a program I initiated, fought for 10 years and has been on-going program listed in the Newsletter in the past. 4). This CPR promotion benefits the citizens to learn to save life and will not benefit my elected position as stated above. 5). Please reply by Fax, my Fax #863-5704 area code 213. Our NewsLetter article deadline for September is 8/18/90 and our CPR anniversary date is Sept. 18th. But we need your interpretation to put this article to promote CPR this week to make it to the print shop. Fax your reply please. Thank you." My phone # is 213-868-6888
